title: Gender Bias in Embedding Math
concept: Analogy math in static embeddings can reflect gender biases (e.g. mapping 'doctor' to male and 'nurse' to female), requiring debiasing algorithms.

exercises.prompt: How do static word embeddings represent the meanings of words?

options.text: By turning words into lists of numbers (vectors)
By matching them to stock photos

exercises.explain: Word embeddings represent words as lists of numbers (vectors) so that computers can calculate how similar words are to each other.

cards.title: Can Math Be Biased?
cards.body: AI models learn about our world by reading millions of pages on the internet. But what happens when the math they use to understand words accidentally copies human prejudices and stereotypes? Let's see how word math can go wrong.

cards.title: Bias in Word Math
cards.body: Gender bias in embedding math happens when computer models learn human stereotypes from text. When we ask the AI to solve word puzzles like 'Man is to Computer Programmer as Woman is to...', it might answer 'Homemaker' instead of 'Computer Programmer' because of biased patterns in its training data.

cards.title: The Dinner Party Seating
cards.body: Imagine word embeddings are like a seating chart for a massive dinner party. We want similar words to sit close together. But if we always seat 'doctor' next to 'man' and 'nurse' next to 'woman' just because of old stereotypes, the seating chart becomes unfair and biased. Debiasing is like rearranging the seats so everyone has an equal opportunity to sit next to any career.

cards.title: Biased Vector Math
cards.body:
In a biased word embedding, doing math with vectors shows unfair links:
• Vector('Doctor') - Vector('Man') + Vector('Woman') = Vector('Nurse')

This math shows the AI incorrectly thinks 'doctor' is inherently male-associated and 'nurse' is female-associated, rather than treating both as neutral careers.

cards.title: Myth: Math is Always Neutral
cards.body: You might think that because math is objective, word vectors cannot be biased. In reality, word embeddings are trained on human-written text. If the training text contains stereotypes, the math will perfectly copy and reinforce those biases!

cards.title: Visualizing the Shift
cards.body: In a biased model, neutral words drift toward gendered words. Debiasing pulls them back to a neutral, fair center.

right.text: Both 'Doctor' and 'Nurse' are neutral and equal distance from gendered words.

left.text: 'Doctor' is closer to 'Man', 'Nurse' is closer to 'Woman'.

exercises.prompt: Why do static word embeddings end up with gender biases?

options.text: Because the computers prefer certain words over others.
Because they learn from human-written text that contains stereotypes.
Because female words are mathematically larger than male words.
Because the math formulas are broken.

exercises.explain: AI models learn from real-world text, which unfortunately contains historical and social biases about jobs and genders.

exercises.prompt: It is possible to use debiasing algorithms to reduce gender bias in word embeddings.
exercises.explain: Word embeddings can be mathematically adjusted (debiased) to reduce stereotypes while keeping the meanings of the words.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about fixing word embeddings.
exercises.sentence: We use _________ algorithms to remove unfair gender stereotypes from word vectors.
exercises.explain: Debiasing algorithms help remove unfair gender shortcuts from word vectors while keeping the useful meanings of the words.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI outputs shows gender bias in its word relationships?

options.text: AI A says: 'A surgeon can be any gender.'
AI B says: 'Man is to Surgeon as Woman is to Nurse.'
Both AI models show the same bias.

exercises.explain: AI B is biased because it associates the neutral job 'surgeon' specifically with one gender, while AI A keeps them neutral.

exercises.prompt: If you want to fix the gender bias in a word embedding so that 'doctor' and 'nurse' are neutral, what is the best approach?

options.text: Delete all gendered words like 'man' and 'woman' from the dictionary.
Force 'doctor' and 'nurse' to have the exact same definition.
Remove the 'gender direction' from neutral words so they sit equally close to both genders.
Only train the AI using data written by computer algorithms.

exercises.explain: If we subtract the direction of gender bias from the embeddings, gender-neutral career words like 'doctor' and 'nurse' end up equally spaced between 'man' and 'woman', removing the unfair stereotypes.
